properties.remark: Webcam at Hartsville showed visibility fell below 500 feet based off cam location to nearby tower that became obscured by snow.
NY state Mesonet cam at Tyrone showed visibilities fell below 400 feet between 9:10 and 9:20 am.

NYSM Scipio Center webcam showed visibility dropped to 700 feet based off of the power lines in webcam and used google maps to map distance. Winds also gusted over 40 mph and road visible in cam became snow covered. Low vis and wind lasted 35 minutes.
NYSM Burdett station showed visibilities falling below 400 feet based off of loss of sight of tree line on webcam. Vis improved after 25 minutes.

NYSM Berkshire camera indicated snow increased in intensity at 955 am with lowest visibilities occurring at 1005 am with visibilities falling to around 400 feet based off of the wood line. Winds gusted to 35 mph, distances were measured using google maps.
Multiple vehicles spun out or disabled on I-81 during a snow squall.
NYSM Groton webcam showed a strong squall at 1005 am as visibility fell well below 600 feet as well as gusts over 40 mph. Distances measured from site location to road with houses.
Car accident on I-81 southbound during a snow squall. Poor visibility and icy roadways at the time.

NYSM Tully webcam showed visibilities dropped below 400 feet based off of buildings in webcam image and distances derived from google maps. Winds also gusted to 38 mph during squall. Lowest visibilities and gusty winds lasted 30 minutes.
Winds gusted over 35 mph and visibility fell below a quarter mile.
NYSM Cincinnatus was hit by several squalls, the first one as at around 1020 when visibilities fell below 700 feet. Visibilities estimated from view of barn and wood line and google maps.
1/8 mile visibility and 30 mph wind gusts observed during a snow squall.
Multiple accidents reported throughout the city due to heavy snow and no visibility.
Cortland ASOS reported visibility below a quarter mile as well as gust to near 35 mph.
RME ASOS had heavy snow dropping visibility below a quarter mile and winds gusting over 30 mph.
0.2 inches of snow was measured and a 39 mph wind gust was report during a snow squall.
NYSM Morrisville webcam showed visibilities fell below 700 feet based off of tree line distance and buildings. Winds also gusted to 38 mph. Distances estimated from google maps.
NTSM Belden webcam showed visibilities fell below 900 feet, estimated using power poles in the image and google maps. Winds gusted to 48 mph as well during the heavy snow.

NYSM Cincinnatus webcam showed visibilities fell below 700 feet along with gust over 30 mph. Distances were estimated from google maps and low vis and wind lasted 20 minutes with noticeable accumulation on the ground.
NYSM Burdett webcam picked up second squall with vis falling back below 500 feet as well as gusts over 40 mph for 10 minutes.
Norwich ASOS reported vis down to a quarter mile as well as 30 mph wind gusts.
2 car accident seen on traffic camera during snow squall.
Oneonta ASOS reported quarter mile vis and 30 mph wind gusts for 10 minutes.
AVP ASOS reported quarter mile visibility and gusts to 46 mph lasting 5 minutes.
Two car accident on I-84 eastbound at mile marker 17 offramp during a snow squall.
Car accident on Hamlin Highway during a snow squall.
Norwich ASOS reported a second squall with quarter mile visibility.
NYSM Walton cam indicated visibilities fell to around 400 feet based off of trees in view of the camera. Google maps was used to estimate visibility. Low vis lasted 15 minutes.

NYSM Eldred camera showed heavy snow with visibility down around 300 feet based off of nearby buildings. Distances were estimated using google maps. Winds gusted to near 40 mph during the squall with noticeable accumulation in a short period of time.
0.6 inches of snow fell during a two snow squalls at NWS Binghamton.
NYSM Cincinnatus recorded a final squall at 1240 pm with vis falling back below 700 feet.
Snow showers and squall resulted in 1.1 inches of snow.
Tree down in yard on East Maine near the town of Maine.

Damage survey confirmed EF1 tornado just south of the hamlet of Castle Creek in the intersection of Brooks Rd and Route 11. Numerous large trees were uprooted and snapped near the base. The tornado tracked from the Sacred Heart of Jesus Ukrainian cemetary to the east, crossing Castle Creek Rd before lifting just west of I-81. A brick chimney was pushed over and damaged the roof of a residence, and two hay wagons were moved over 100 feet and destroyed. The side of an old barn was defaced and several wood fragments were embedded into the ground.
Tree down across Brooks Rd along with chimney knocked off roof.
Several trees down in front of home in Castle Creek.
Squall lasted for over 10 minutes with approximately 0.1 inches of snow, less than 1/4 mile visibility & 30 mph wind gusts.

Corrects previous tornado report from West Damascus. A brief tornado EF1 tornado with 90mph maximum winds touched down near the intersection of Oregon Turnpike and Cochecton Turnpike in West Damascus in the early evening of May 27th. The tornado initially produced minor tree and crop damage and lifted some debris into powerlines. It then continued eastward and crossed Cochecton Turnpike as well as Rutledgedale Road, uprooted a very large tree, and lofted additional debris into the tree. The tornado then crossed through a farm and caused significant roof and structural damage to a couple of barns. This is where the tornado was at its peak strength, with a metal roof partially lifted off a barn and a large wooden door ripped off another barn. Debris was also lofted down a hill roughly 100 feet away. Some minor tree and crop damage was observed down this hill, which was the last visible damage of the survey and the determined location where the tornado lifted.
NWS Storm Survey confirms EF1 tornado with maximum winds of 90 mph.

An intense microburst occurred in a wooded higher elevation location of southwestern Schuyler County about 2 miles NNW of Monterey. In addition to dozens of uprooted treesup to 2 feet in diameter, there was a focused corridor of large trunk snaps, both hardwood and softwood. Damage though intense was limited to trees. The damage pattern was immediately intense at the initial microburst impact and then diverged while dissipating across Switzer Hill Road. Other minor tree damage could be found up and down the road outside of the main microburst corridor.

Significant tree damage, including snapped trees. Trees on several mobile homes, including 6-8 mobile homes that are likely destroyed. No injuries.

A downburst heavily damaged and uprooted a grove of large trees up to 2 feet in diameter including sycamores. They fell in a divergent pattern, indicating a downburst. Many trees or large branches fell on several mobile homes, entrapping 3 residents, while also crushing 2 vehicles and a riding mower. On the front side of the microburst, a Department of Public Works storage building was completely blown apart with only one wall left standing.

An EF-0 tornado touched down near Daisy Hollow Drive in Virgil NY. The tornado tracked east northeast along Babcock Hollow Road, passed near Greek Peak Resort, and lifted near Route 11 just south of Cortlandville. A few homes sustained minor damage to their roofs, with tree damage being the most prominent amount of damage.

A microburst occurred in South Otselic in Chenango County NY on the evening of July 15, 2024. Estimated peak wind was 80 mph. The majority of damage was tree damage.

NWS survey confirms embedded EF1 tornado spun up within a kink in a line of severe thunderstorms. The tornado took a meandering and at times discontinuous path through rugged terrain. Its peak 110 mph intensity occurred along a ridge of Joe Road, with hundreds of uprooted and snapped large softwood trees in a swath over 200 yards wide. Tree snaps and uproots continued across County Road 13 and Ridge Road, before dropping into the Otselic Valley. A barn was partially collapsed, and another had a roof displaced. Other structures had minor roofing or window damage from Otselic Center to Otselic itself at the end around 702 PM EDT.

*** 1 FATAL *** A tornado touched down between the Conrail railroad tracks and Barlow Street. The tornado was witnessed by town officials, residents and a video security camera on Canal Street as it moved to the east along Barlow and Railroad Streets. Severe damage occurred to multiple residences along Barlow Street with significant loss of roofs and roofing material as well as blowing out garage doors and windows. Two sheds were destroyed and multiple large diameter tree trunks and limbs were snapped. An 82 year old male succumbed to injuries sustained while he was outside as the tornado crossed Main Street. The tornado continued down Center Street causing further damage to trees, roofs and a few power poles. The tornado veered slightly to the northeast crossing the Old Erie Canal, causing additional tree damage and tearing a metal roof off of a pole barn along Canal Road. It then weakened and dissipated before striking a solar farm which sustained nearly no damage. The damage found along most of the tornado track was consistent with maximum wind speeds around 100 mph which is within the EF-1 category of the Enhanced Fujita scale.

NWS Survey confirmed an EF1 tornado in the Town of Lee, starting at Palmer Road. It strengthened while tracking across Streun, Point Rock, Harris, and Capron roads where structures had roof damage from winds or falling trees. A mobile home shifted off of its blocks. It progressed over Podunk Road, crossing Route 26 and tearing off half of a roof. The tornado ended along Beartown Road, just over the Town of Western line at 330 PM EDT.
Destruction of church steeple, and ripped of church roof. Roof ripped off of grocery store. Time estimated based off radar.

A high-end EF2 tornado touched down near Muck Road of Rome NY, and lifted just past Griffiss International Airport. Estimated max wind speeds of 135 mph. Two churches sustained significant damage to their roofs and steeples. Two large brick buildings sustained major damage to their roofs, with collapsed walls. Spray damage from these buildings caused numerous crushed vehicles. A vehicle was also flipped in a parking lot. Hundreds of large trees were snapped or uprooted. Multiple properties experienced significant losses of roofs or roofing material, blown out garages and windows, as well as significant damage to exterior walls including being impaled by missiles in the form of branches or other objects.
